editableNewDialogue.text: If you can’t open links, what’s the best way to get help describing a historical photo?

Copy the text that appears near the photo (caption, date, source) and describe what you see: clothing, insignia, facial hair, pose, background, and any visible writing. If you want, you can paste a short list like “uniform color, hat type, shoulder boards, medals, ship/port in background,” and I’ll help turn it into a structured description and likely time/place clues.
Can you also give a small Ukrainian vocabulary list for describing a navy serviceman in an old portrait?

Sure—here are useful Ukrainian words/phrases: матрос (sailor), моряк (seaman), військовослужбовець (serviceman), флот (navy/fleet), Балтійський флот (Baltic Fleet), форма/мундир (uniform), кашкет/безкозирка (cap/sailor cap), погони (shoulder boards), кітель (tunic/jacket), комір (collar), ґудзики (buttons), відзнаки/нашивки (insignia/patches), нагорода/медаль (award/medal), орден (order/decoration), портрет (portrait), світлина/фотографія (photo), підпис (caption/signature), дата знімка (date of the shot).
How would you phrase a neutral caption in Ukrainian if the exact identity isn’t fully confirmed?

You can use cautious wording like: «Ймовірно, [ім’я/прізвище], у військово-морській формі. Фото, імовірно, періоду [роки], [місце/флот], точна атрибуція потребує підтвердження». Or shorter: «Невстановлений моряк у формі; можливе датування — [рік/діапазон]».

I tried that wooden Cossack saber thing again and Pikach Dude just stared at me, then ran under the kitchen table when I yelled "Oinos!"—so I put the saber away. Anyway, about those Ukrainian words from that Baltic-fleet photo/article: can you give me a short list I can use for a glossary?

If he’s retreating under the table, it’s a good call to stop that kind of waving/yelling around him.

For a glossary-style list from the kind of Ukrainian text you quoted (military + local authority + 1917–1919 context), here are common words/terms you can use, with simple English glosses:
- отаман — ataman, Cossack leader
- вільне козацтво — Free Cossacks
- волость — volost (local district/administrative unit)
- повіт — county/district (uezd)
- губернія — губернія (province)
- Центральна Рада — Central Rada
- Директорія — Directory (government leadership)
- визвольні змагання — liberation struggle
- мобілізація — mobilization
- реквізиція — requisition
- загін — detachment/unit
- похід — campaign/expedition
- флот — fleet
- Балтійський флот — Baltic Fleet
- нагорода — award/decoration
- селяни — peasants
- збройний — armed
- засідка — ambush
If you paste a paragraph from the article text you’re using, I can tailor the glossary to the exact wording.
About that dog thing—I wrote it messy before. I don’t own a Dachshund-Pomeranian-German Hound. I only have a male Dachshund–Pomeranian mix, and his name is “Pikach Dude”.
Got it. So the correct detail is: you have a male Dachshund–Pomeranian mix named “Pikach Dude,” and there isn’t a German Hound in his breed mix.

turns.text: About that dog thing—I wrote it messy before. I don’t own a Dachshund-Pomeranian-German Hound. I only have a male Dachshund–Pomeranian mix, and his name is “Pikach Dude”.
